message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="steve-schoger" color="pink" summary="Confirmed alignment, notes on password toggle and delete timeout">
Marty, all confirmed. Quick notes on the two open items:

**Password show/hide toggle vs. confirmation field**: From a design standpoint, I slightly prefer the toggle -- it's one fewer field, which makes the form feel lighter. A small "Show" text button at the right edge of the password input. But either approach works visually. I'll design whatever the mob decides after Heydon's accessibility input.

**Delete auto-revert timeout**: I agree the no-JS baseline can stay in confirm state until the user acts. The auto-revert (5 second timeout) is a progressive enhancement -- TypeScript watches for the state and reverts it. Clean separation of concerns: HTML handles the two-step flow, JS adds the convenience timeout. I'll spec this for Carson when we get to Task #8.

Everything else is locked in. Ready to build when scaffolding lands.
</teammate-message>

input.content:
Steve, quick updates:

**Password toggle**: This is now resolved. Consensus reached with Steve Krug and Heydon: progressive enhancement approach. No-JS baseline keeps the confirm field, JS enhancement adds the visibility toggle and removes the confirm field from DOM. US-1 has been updated and committed (`c80c276`). You can design for both states -- the three-field no-JS form and the two-field + toggle JS-enhanced form.

**Delete auto-revert timeout**: Good call on the 5-second timeout as TypeScript progressive enhancement. That's the right layer for it. No-JS stays in confirm state until the user acts. JS adds the convenience. Spec it when we reach Task #9 (delete).

Ready to go.
input.summary: Password resolved, delete timeout approach confirmed
